name: add radon to block python complexity
add new alias for assist

i can start the program with assist

name: jb inspect
description:
we can use `jb inspect` to find dead c# code in the files we've changed:

```
  dotnet tool install -g JetBrains.ReSharper.GlobalTools
  jb inspectcode YourSolution.sln -o=report.xml --include="src/Path/To/ChangedFile.cs"
```

We should add this as a dotnet command to assist.

name: Add refactor extract command
acceptanceCriteria: Extracts the named function and all same-file private (non-exported) functions it transitively depends on
Creates the destination file with the extracted functions and their required imports
Removes the extracted functions from the source file
Adds import { functionName } from "./destination" to the source file
Updates any other files that already import the function (if it was exported)
Adds the export to the nearest barrel index.ts if one exists in the destination directory
Dry-run by default showing what would happen, --apply to execute
assist verify passes after extraction with --apply
description: Add assist refactor extract <file> <functionName> <destination> that uses ts-morph to move a function and its private dependencies from one file to a new file, updating all imports. Follows existing refactor patterns (dry-run by default, --apply to execute).

tasks.task: Find the target function declaration in the source file using ts-morph
Walk the same-file call graph to collect all private (non-exported) functions the target transitively depends on
Resolve the required imports for the destination file (imports used by extracted functions)
Create the destination file with extracted functions and their required imports
Remove extracted functions from the source file
Add import of the extracted function to the source file
Update any other files that already import the function
Add export to nearest barrel index.ts if one exists in the destination directory
Register the extract command under refactor with --apply flag
Update README.md with new command documentation
Update CLAUDE.md refactor docs to mention extract command
Add read-only command to settings.json allow list

name: Display plan and phase progress in backlog web view

name: Auto-generate review phase for backlog items
acceptanceCriteria: When a backlog item with a plan is added (via --json or interactive), a review phase is auto-appended
The review phase prompt instructs the agent to verify each acceptance criterion against the current code
The agent reports pass/fail per criterion before proceeding
The agent asks the user to confirm manual checks
After confirmation, the agent runs assist backlog done <id>, then /commit, then assist backlog phase-done
The review phase is always the last phase and is not editable by the author
Existing items without a review phase are unaffected (no migration)
description: Automatically append a review phase to every backlog item when added. The review phase verifies all acceptance criteria against the implementation, asks the user to perform manual checks, marks the story done, calls /commit, then signals phase-done.

tasks.task: Create a helper function that generates the review phase object (name: Review, with a single task describing the review process)
Modify addFromJson in add/index.ts to append the review phase to items that have a plan
Interactive add does not add plans so no change needed there — add a comment clarifying this
Add a detection mechanism in buildPhasePrompt to identify the review phase (e.g. by name convention)
Build a specialised prompt for the review phase that lists each AC and instructs the agent to verify against code
Prompt instructs the agent to: confirm manual checks, run assist backlog done, call /commit, then call phase-done
Update run.ts completion message since the review phase now handles commit
Update README.md with documentation about the auto-generated review phase

name: refactor extract leaves unused imports in source file
acceptanceCriteria: After extracting a function, any imports in the source file that are no longer referenced by remaining code are removed
Extraction does not remove imports that are still used by other code in the source file
description:
**Repro:**
1. Have a file (`run.ts`) with a function (`resolvePlan`) that is the sole consumer of certain type imports (`BacklogItem`, `PlanPhase`)
2. Run `assist refactor extract src/commands/backlog/run.ts resolvePlan src/commands/backlog/resolvePlan.ts --apply`
3. Inspect the source file after extraction

**Expected:** Imports that are no longer used by any remaining code in the source file are removed during extraction.

**Actual:** The `import type { BacklogItem, PlanPhase } from "./types"` was left behind in `run.ts` despite `resolvePlan` being the only function that referenced those types. This caused a lint failure (`noUnusedImports`).

name: backlog add --json fails when echo passes JSON with \n literals
acceptanceCriteria: An agent can add a backlog item with multi-line description fields in a single attempt without hitting a JSON parse error
description:
**Repro:**
1. An agent generates JSON with `\n` escape sequences in string values
2. Pipes it to `assist backlog add --json` using `echo`
3. `JSON.parse` throws `SyntaxError: Bad control character in string literal`

**Expected:** The agent successfully adds a backlog item on the first attempt.

**Actual:** The command fails, requiring the agent to retry with a different shell approach (heredoc).

name: refactor extract adds unused barrel export to index.ts
acceptanceCriteria: refactor extract only adds a barrel export when the function was already exported via the barrel before extraction
Extracting a non-barrel-exported function does not modify index.ts
description:
**Repro:**
1. Have a file with a private helper function (e.g. `handleIncompletePhase` in `resolvePhaseResult.ts`) that is only used internally within the same directory
2. Run `assist refactor extract <source> <functionName> <destination> --apply`
3. Check the directory's `index.ts` barrel file

**Expected:** The extracted function should only be exported from the new file and imported where needed. No barrel export should be added unless the function was already publicly exported.

**Actual:** The extract command unconditionally adds a barrel export to `index.ts` (e.g. `export { handleIncompletePhase } from "./handleIncompletePhase"`), even when the function is only consumed internally. This causes knip to flag an unused export.

name: refactor extract copies import paths verbatim into destination
acceptanceCriteria: When extracting to a different directory, import paths in the destination file are rewritten relative to the destination
Build and type checks pass on the extracted file without manual fixup
description:
**Repro:**
1. Have a file `src/commands/registerBacklog.ts` that imports from `"./backlog"` (a barrel)
2. Run `assist refactor extract src/commands/registerBacklog.ts registerItemCommands src/commands/backlog/registerItemCommands.ts --apply`
3. The extracted file lands inside `backlog/` but still contains `from "./backlog"`

**Expected:** Import paths in the extracted file are rewritten relative to the destination directory (e.g. direct imports from `./add`, `./init`, `./list`).

**Actual:** Import paths are copied verbatim from the source file, producing an unresolvable `./backlog` import that fails build and type checks.

name: Fix cli-hook check failing to parse glob patterns
acceptanceCriteria: assist cli-hook check with glob patterns (e.g. *.ts) no longer returns unable to parse
Glob tokens are converted to their pattern string in splitCompound
Existing tests still pass; new test covers glob handling
description: splitCompound rejects commands containing glob patterns (e.g. *.ts) because shell-quote parses them as { op: "glob", pattern: "..." } tokens, which are not strings. These should be treated as safe argument values.

tasks.task: Update groupByOperator in splitCompound.ts to treat glob tokens as their pattern string
Add test cases for glob patterns in splitCompound tests

name: Fix run add to split inline command args at save time
acceptanceCriteria: assist run add verify:lint "npx biome check --diagnostic-level=error" stores command: npx and args: [biome, check, --diagnostic-level=error]
setupVerifyRunEntry.ts uses the same shared buildRunEntry
Runtime split workaround removed from buildCommand in index.ts
Tests cover: no-space command, space-in-command split, and separate args passthrough
description: assist run add stores a quoted multi-word command as a single string with no args. assist verify then fails because buildFullCommand in resolveEntries.ts does not split at runtime. Fix by splitting at save time in add.ts, unifying with the split logic already in setupVerifyRunEntry.ts.

tasks.task: Extract buildRunEntry to src/commands/run/buildRunEntry.ts with whitespace splitting when args is empty
Update add.ts to import from the new file
Update setupVerifyRunEntry.ts to use the shared function, adding GLOBAL_COMMANDS/npx logic on top
Remove the runtime split workaround from buildCommand in index.ts
Add src/commands/run/buildRunEntry.test.ts covering no-split, split, and separate-args cases

name: Add --dir option to backlog commands
acceptanceCriteria: backlog --dir /some/path list reads the backlog from /some/path/assist.backlog.yml
backlog --dir /some/path add --json writes to /some/path/assist.backlog.yml
backlog --dir /some/path run <id> writes the phase status marker to /some/path/.assist-phase-status.json
When --dir is not provided, behavior is unchanged (uses cwd)
The top-level next alias also respects --dir if passed
description: Add a --dir option to the backlog parent command that overrides the directory used for backlog file discovery and marker file resolution. When provided, all backlog operations resolve assist.backlog.yml and .assist-phase-status.json from the given directory instead of process.cwd(). Config discovery is unaffected.

comments.text: Phase 1: Added --dir option via preAction hook on backlog parent command. Introduced getBacklogDir/setBacklogDir in shared.ts. Updated getSignalPath and getLockPath to use getBacklogDir. Extracted readStdin and registerStatusCommands to fix maintainability.

Plumbed --dir through backlog commands: added setBacklogDir/getBacklogDir to shared.ts, wired preAction hook on backlog parent command, updated getSignalPath and getLockPath to use getBacklogDir instead of process.cwd(). Extracted readStdin and registerStatusCommands to maintain complexity thresholds.
Updated README.md with --dir option on backlog parent command. Top-level next alias left unchanged per user direction.
Added --dir option to backlog parent command via preAction hook and module-level backlogDir override in shared.ts

tasks.task: Add --dir option to the backlog parent command in registerBacklog.ts
Refactor getBacklogPath() and getPhaseStatusPath() to accept an optional directory parameter and thread it through all callers (shared helpers, web, phaseDone, etc.)
Wire --dir through the top-level next alias
Update README.md with the new --dir option

If the repo has no git or the commit fails (e.g. dirty index conflicts), the item is still added — the commit is best-effort with a warning
Interactive and --json add paths both trigger the commit

tasks.task: After saveBacklog() in add/index.ts, git add the backlog file and commit it with message chore: add backlog item #<id> — <name>. Handle errors gracefully (warn but do not fail). Ensure both the interactive and --json code paths are covered.

acceptanceCriteria: assist backlog comment <id> <text> adds a comment to the item
assist backlog phase-done <id> <phase> <summary> accepts a required summary argument
Comments are stored in a comments array on the backlog item with { text, phase?, timestamp, type: summary | comment }
assist backlog comments <id> lists all comments/summaries for an item
Phase prompts tell the agent it can run assist backlog comments <id> to read prior phase notes, and that it can run assist backlog comment <id> <text> to post findings or changes
Phase prompts instruct the agent to provide a summary when calling phase-done
`assist backlog web` displays comments/summaries on the item detail screen
`assist backlog show <id>` (#23) displays comments if present — update show command to render comments when this lands first
description: Allow agents to post comments against backlog items at any time for findings or changes worth noting, and require a concise phase summary when completing a phase. Both are stored as annotations on the item, visible via CLI, and available for other phases to read on demand.

comments.text: Added comment, comments, and phase-done summary commands. Registered in registerBacklog.ts, updated show to display comments, extracted formatComment helper to avoid duplication, split registerBacklog into smaller functions for maintainability.

Added CommentsSection component to web UI. Updated UI types to include BacklogComment, created CommentsSection.tsx with styled cards for comments and summaries (amber for summaries, gray for comments), and wired it into ItemBody after the plan section.

tasks.task: Add comments array to backlog item schema in types.ts with { text, phase?, timestamp, type: summary | comment }
Add helper functions to append a comment/summary entry in shared.ts
Add backlog comment <id> <text> command that appends a comment entry
Update phase-done to accept and store a required summary argument
Add backlog comments <id> list command that displays all comments/summaries
Register all new commands in registerBacklog.ts

name: CLI launch modes for draft and bug with /next chaining
acceptanceCriteria: .assist-phase-status.json is renamed to a generic signal file (e.g. .assist-signal.json) with an event-type field
Existing phase-done flow works as before using the new file format
assist draft spawns claude /draft with default permissions, watching for the signal file
assist bug spawns claude /bug with default permissions, watching for the signal file
/next slash command writes the signal file, causing the watcher to terminate Claude
After termination via /next, assist immediately runs assist next (the full backlog-next flow)
Signal file is cleaned up between sessions
description: Add assist draft and assist bug CLI commands that spawn Claude Code in the respective slash command mode, watching for a generic signal file. Introduce a /next slash command that writes the signal to terminate the session and chain into assist next. Rename .assist-phase-status.json to a generic signal file used for all assist-Claude communication.

comments.text: Renamed .assist-phase-status.json to .assist-signal.json with event-type field. Updated phaseDone to write event: phase-done, renamed getPhaseStatusPath to getSignalPath, updated watchForMarker, resolvePhaseResult, .gitignore, README, and tests.

Added /next slash command, assist draft/bug CLI commands with signal-based chaining. Extracted writeSignal/readSignal helpers, moved getSignalPath to writeSignal.ts to avoid circular deps. On /next signal, Claude is terminated and assist next runs automatically.

Fixed session isolation bug: concurrent processes shared a single signal file, causing cross-talk. writeSignal now includes sessionId from ASSIST_SESSION_ID env var; watchForMarker only reacts to matching signals.
Implemented CLI launch modes (assist draft, assist bug) with /next chaining via session-scoped signal file. Fixed concurrent process isolation by embedding sessionId in signals.

tasks.task: Rename .assist-phase-status.json to a generic signal file name (e.g. .assist-signal.json) with an event-type field in the JSON payload
Update phaseDone to write the new file format with event type
Update watchForMarker and resolvePhaseResult to read the new file format
Update .gitignore and any other references to the old filename

On signal detection, terminate Claude and chain into assist next

name: Global config support for sync auto-confirm
acceptanceCriteria: assist config set --global sync.autoConfirm true writes to ~/.assist.yml
assist sync reads sync.autoConfirm from config and skips prompts when true
assist update no longer hardcodes --yes; it calls assist sync and lets the config decide
assist config set sync.autoConfirm true (without --global) errors or warns since it is a global-only key
Default is false (existing behavior preserved)
description: Add a sync.autoConfirm config key (global-only) that controls whether assist sync skips confirmation prompts. Extend assist config set with a --global flag to write to ~/.assist.yml. Wire assist update and assist sync to read this setting.

comments.text: Added sync.autoConfirm (default false) to assistConfigSchema. Added --global flag to config set that writes via saveGlobalConfig. Extracted configGet to its own file to maintain code quality. Added tests for global and project config set paths.

Wired sync to read sync.autoConfirm from config (falling back to false). Removed hardcoded --yes from update's assist sync calls. Added tests for both sync config-reading and update command invocation.
Added sync.autoConfirm global-only config key with --global flag on config set. Wired sync and update to read the setting. Added global-only key guard preventing project-level writes.

tasks.task: Add sync schema to assistConfigSchema with autoConfirm boolean defaulting to false
Add --global flag to assist config set that writes via saveGlobalConfig
Add tests for global config set
Update sync to read sync.autoConfirm from config and pass it as the yes option
Update update to call assist sync without --yes, relying on config
Add tests for both paths

name: Backlog ls alias
acceptanceCriteria: assist backlog ls works identically to assist backlog list
All options (--status, -a, -v) work with the alias

comments.text: Added .alias('ls') to the list command in registerItemCommands.ts
Added .alias('ls') to backlog list command in registerItemCommands.ts

name: Loop assist next when backlog items remain
acceptanceCriteria: After completing a backlog item, assist next loops back into next() with existing selection logic (prompt if multiple, auto-pick if one)
When no todo items remain, prints a message and exits
If the user exits Claude without a signal being written (abort/no signal), the loop stops
description: After assist next finishes running an item, it should loop back and call next again if there are remaining todo items, preserving the existing selection behaviour (auto-pick if one item, prompt if multiple). If no items remain, print a message and exit. The loop breaks if the user exits without a signal being written.

comments.text: Wrapped next() in a while loop that re-loads backlog after each run() completion. run() now returns boolean to distinguish completion from abort. Loop continues on success, breaks on abort/no-signal, prints completion message when no todo items remain.

Wrapped next() in a while(true) loop that re-loads backlog after each run(). run() returns boolean to distinguish completion from abort. Loop continues on success, breaks on abort/no-signal, prints completion message when no todo items remain.

tasks.task: Wrap the core logic of next() in a loop. After run() completes successfully, re-enter the loop (which re-loads the backlog and applies existing selection logic). If run() returns without completing (abort), break. If no todo items remain, print a completion message and break.

name: Test backlog item
acceptanceCriteria: Item can be added to the backlog
Item appears in assist backlog list
description: A simple test item to verify the backlog workflow works correctly.

comments.text: Logged success message confirming the test backlog item ran successfully. No code changes needed — this phase was purely a workflow verification.
Verified backlog workflow: item was added and appears in list. No code changes required.

tasks.task: Log a message confirming the test item ran successfully

name: Concurrent assist next agents compete for the same in-progress item
acceptanceCriteria: An in-progress item actively being worked on by another agent is skipped
An in-progress item with no active agent is still resumable
Two concurrent assist next invocations work on different items
description:
**Repro:**
1. Have a backlog with at least two todo items
2. Run `assist next` — it picks an item and sets it to in-progress
3. In another terminal, run `assist next` again

**Expected:** The second agent skips the item already being worked on and picks the next todo item.

**Actual:** The second agent sees the in-progress item and tries to resume it, competing with the first agent.

**Edge case:** If both agents are terminated without completing, a fresh `assist next` should still pick up one of the abandoned in-progress items.

comments.text: Added PID-based lock files (.assist-lock-{id}.json) so concurrent assist next agents skip items actively worked on by another process. Stale locks (dead PID) are ignored, keeping abandoned items resumable.
PID-based lock files (.assist-lock-{id}.json) prevent concurrent assist next agents from competing for the same item. Stale locks (dead PID) are ignored so abandoned items remain resumable.

name: assist next crashes on Ctrl-C during story prompt
acceptanceCriteria: Pressing Ctrl-C during any interactive prompt in `assist next` exits cleanly without printing an error
description:
**Repro:**
1. Run `assist next`
2. When prompted to select/confirm a story, press Ctrl-C

**Expected:** CLI exits gracefully with no output (exit code 0 or 130).

**Actual:** Unhandled promise rejection error is printed to stderr and process exits with a non-zero code.

comments.text: Added exitOnCancel wrapper that catches enquirer Ctrl-C rejections and calls process.exit(0). Applied to all prompt call sites: next.ts select prompt, handleIncompletePhase.ts, promptConfirm, promptInput, promptPassword, and promptMultiselect. Extracted selectItem helper from next.ts to satisfy maintainability threshold.
Added exitOnCancel wrapper for enquirer prompts so Ctrl-C exits cleanly (exit 0) instead of printing an unhandled rejection.

name: Support PowerShell permissions in cli-hook
acceptanceCriteria: assist verify (and all other allowed commands) auto-approve when run via the PowerShell tool
Denied commands (git commit, npm run, npx assist) are also denied via PowerShell
Existing Bash behaviour is unchanged
description: The cli-hook only handles Bash tool calls. Since Claude Code now has a PowerShell tool, the hook needs to process PowerShell commands too by merging allow/deny rules across both tools.

comments.text: Phase 1 complete: matchesBashAllow renamed to matchesAllow.ts with matchesAllow(toolName, cmd) and matchesDeny(toolName, cmd) parsing both Bash() and PowerShell() entries. cliHook accepts both tools and checks deny before allow. PowerShell hook matcher and deny rules added to settings.json.

Made cli-hook tool-agnostic: shellPrefixes() merges Bash/PowerShell allow and deny rules so a single entry covers both tools. Removed redundant PowerShell config entries from settings.json. Added cross-tool matching tests.

name: Deny rules with correction messages
acceptanceCriteria: assistConfigSchema accepts a deny array of { pattern, message } objects
assist cli-hook checks commands against deny rules using prefix matching before allow checks
Denied commands return permissionDecision deny with the message in permissionDecisionReason
Deny rules take precedence over allow rules
assist cli-hook check <command> reports deny matches
description: Add a deny config section to assist.yml that lets users define command patterns with correction messages. When the cli-hook matches a denied command, it returns a deny decision with the message so the agent self-corrects without human intervention.

comments.text: Phase 1: Added deny array schema (pattern + message) to assistConfigSchema as optional field. loadConfig passes rules through via existing Zod parse. 5 unit tests cover valid config, omission, and validation of missing/extra fields.
Added deny array schema ({pattern, message}) to assistConfigSchema in types.ts with unit tests confirming validation and loadConfig passthrough

Phase 2: Added matchesConfigDeny for prefix-matching commands against assist.yml deny rules. Integrated into cli-hook (resolvePermission checks config deny before settings deny and allow) and cli-hook check. Compound commands are split and each sub-command checked. Extracted tryParseJson/extractCommand/findDeny helpers to keep maintainability above 70.

Integrated config deny rules into cli-hook enforcement. Commands are checked against assist.yml deny rules (prefix matching) before settings deny and allow rules. Compound commands (pipes, &&, ||) have each sub-command checked. cli-hook check reports deny matches with the configured message. Added matchesConfigDeny module and tests for all paths.
Phase 3 tests: 18 tests across 4 files covering schema validation, matchesConfigDeny prefix matching, cliHook deny integration, and cliHookCheck deny output. All verify checks pass.

Added 18 tests across 4 files: schema validation (5), matchesConfigDeny prefix matching (5), cliHook deny integration (4), and cliHookCheck deny output (4). Covers deny matching, precedence over allow, compound commands, and check output.

All 5 acceptance criteria verified via code review and passing tests. Config deny rules checked before allow in both cli-hook and cli-hook check paths. Prefix matching works correctly with word boundary protection.
Deny rules with correction messages: schema, prefix matching, deny-before-allow precedence in cli-hook, cli-hook check reporting, and CLI commands (cli-hook deny add/remove/list)

Ensure loadConfig passes deny rules through correctly

name: Add --from option to seq query
acceptanceCriteria: seq query accepts a --from option with a date or datetime string
The value is passed as the fromDateUtc parameter to the Seq /api/events endpoint
Omitting --from preserves existing behaviour (no date filter)
description: The seq query command does not support a time range, so events outside the Seq API's default window are missed. Add a --from option that maps to the fromDateUtc parameter on the Seq /api/events endpoint, allowing queries like `assist seq query "JobId == ..." --from 2026-03-30`.

comments.text: Phase 1: Added --from option to seq query, mapped to fromDateUtc API param. Extracted fetchSeqEvents to maintain code quality threshold.
Added --from option to seq query command, passing it as fromDateUtc to the Seq API. Extracted fetchSeqEvents helper to keep maintainability index above threshold.

Review: all 3 acceptance criteria pass. --from option wired through registerSeq, conditionally sets fromDateUtc param, omitting preserves existing behaviour. fetchSeqEvents extracted cleanly. All verify checks green.
Added --from option to seq query, mapping to fromDateUtc on the Seq API. Extracted fetchSeqEvents for cleaner separation.

name: Use /api/data endpoint for historical seq queries
acceptanceCriteria: When --from is specified, seq query uses /api/data with a SQL query instead of /api/events
The filter expression is translated to SQL WHERE clause (single-quoted strings, Seq SQL syntax)
Results are formatted the same as /api/events output
Without --from, existing /api/events behaviour is preserved
description:
The Seq /api/events endpoint scans backwards from "now" with a limited scan depth. Even with fromDateUtc, it fails to reach events more than a few hours old. This is a known Seq limitation (see https://github.com/datalust/seq-tickets/discussions/2025).

The /api/data endpoint (SQL query engine) performs a proper indexed search and reliably returns historical events. When --from is specified, seq query should use /api/data instead of /api/events.

Example: `GET /api/data?q=select * from stream where JobId = 'xxx' limit 50&fromDateUtc=2026-03-30T00:00:00.000Z`

comments.text: Phase 1: When --from is specified, seq query now uses /api/data with SQL instead of /api/events. Filter expressions are translated (== to =, double-quoted to single-quoted strings). Response rows are mapped back to SeqEvent format so output is identical. Extracted shared fetch logic to fetchSeq.ts to avoid duplication between the two endpoints.

Implemented /api/data endpoint for historical seq queries: routes to SQL-based /api/data when --from is specified, translates filter expressions to SQL WHERE clauses, maps columnar response to SeqEvent format for consistent output, preserves existing /api/events behaviour without --from
Fixed during review: Seq /api/data returns Columns as string[] (not {Name}[]) and timestamps as .NET ticks (not ISO). Updated type, added ticksToIso converter, and fixed tests.

Use /api/data endpoint for historical seq queries. When --from is specified, seq query now uses /api/data with SQL instead of /api/events. Filter expressions are translated to SQL WHERE clauses. Fixed column type and .NET ticks timestamp conversion discovered during e2e testing.

name: PowerShell commands prompt for approval despite Bash allow rules
acceptanceCriteria: PowerShell invocations of allowed commands (e.g. assist verify) auto-approve without prompting
The assist sync output includes PowerShell(...) allow entries alongside the existing Bash(...) ones
Deny rules also cover both tools
cli-hook check accepts an optional --tool flag (defaulting to Bash) so both tools can be verified independently
description:
**Repro:**
1. Open a project that has `Bash(assist verify:*)` (and similar) in `.claude/settings.json` allow list but no corresponding `PowerShell(...)` entries
2. Run `assist cli-hook check "assist verify"` — reports the command is allowed
3. Have Claude Code invoke `PowerShell(assist verify powershell 2>&1)`

**Expected:** The command auto-approves — #34 made the cli-hook cross-tool so Bash allow rules should cover PowerShell too.

**Actual:** Claude Code's built-in permission check runs before the cli-hook and finds no matching `PowerShell(...)` entry, so it prompts for approval. The cli-hook's cross-tool merging never gets a chance to run. The `cli-hook check` command gives a false positive because it only tests hook logic, not the settings permission entries that Claude Code evaluates first.

comments.text: Phase 1: expandShellPerms in syncSettings auto-generates PowerShell(...) entries from Bash(...) ones during sync. cli-hook check now accepts --tool flag (defaults to Bash). Deny rules also expanded for both tools.

Added expandShellPerms to syncSettings that auto-generates PowerShell(...) entries alongside Bash(...) for both allow and deny rules. Added --tool flag to cli-hook check (defaults to Bash). Updated README.

MAJOR ISSUE: expandShellPerms duplicates every Bash permission as a PowerShell entry in claude/settings.json — this bloats the settings file and is explicitly unwanted. The correct approach: no 'assist' commands should appear in claude/settings.json allow section at all. They should be added to assist.cli-reads instead, where the cli-hook already handles cross-tool approval. Need a test asserting no assist* entries exist in settings.json allow.

New requirement: no 'assist' commands should appear in claude/settings.json in the allow section. They should be added to assist.cli-reads. Add a test that asserts there are no assist* entries in settings.json.

Moved assist commands from settings.json allow list to assist.cli-reads so the cli-hook handles cross-tool (Bash/PowerShell) approval automatically. Removed expandShellPerms duplication approach. Added test asserting no assist entries in settings.json.

name: Backlog item linking
acceptanceCriteria: Items can have a `links` array with entries specifying type (`relates-to` | `depends-on`) and target item ID
`assist backlog link <from> <to> --type relates-to|depends-on` creates a link
`assist backlog unlink <from> <to>` removes a link between two items
`assist backlog show <id>` displays the item's links with target name and status
`assist backlog list` indicates when items have dependencies
Adding a `depends-on` link that would create a circular dependency is rejected with an error
Linking to a non-existent item is rejected with an error
description: Add the ability to link backlog items together with "relates-to" (informational) and "depends-on" (directional dependency) relationship types, with circular dependency detection.

Phase 2: Implemented link/unlink commands with validation (target exists, no self-links, no duplicates, circular dep detection for depends-on). Extracted hasCycle and validateLinkTarget for maintainability.
Implemented backlog link/unlink commands with full validation: target existence, self-link prevention, duplicate detection, and circular dependency detection for depends-on links
Added links section to backlog show (type, target name, status) and dependency count indicator to backlog list. Extracted printLinks and printPhaseTasks to maintain complexity threshold.

Concurrent writes to different items no longer race (row-level updates)

Implement importFromJsonl() that reads JSONL and upserts into DB, called on read when JSONL mtime is newer than last-import timestamp stored in DB metadata

name: refactor extract removes shared helpers from source file
acceptanceCriteria: Extracting a function that shares private helpers with remaining code does not break the source file
description:
**Repro:**
1. Have a file with exported function `A` and private helpers `h1`, `h2`, `h3` used by both `A` and other functions in the file
2. Run `assist refactor extract <file> A <destination> --apply`

**Expected:** Shared helpers are not silently removed from the source file.

**Actual:** Helpers are moved to the destination file and removed from the source, breaking all remaining functions that depend on them.

comments.text: Fix: collectDependencies now partitions dep functions into toCopy/toRemove (mirroring the existing statement logic). Shared helpers are copied to the destination but kept in the source. Also fixed statement removal to account for statements used by shared helpers that stay.

Partition dep functions into toCopy/toRemove so shared helpers are copied to destination but preserved in source. Extracted getFunctionMap and collectPrivateStatements to separate files for maintainability.

Review PASS: collectDependencies correctly distinguishes toCopy from toRemove for both functions and statements. Shared helpers are copied to destination but retained in source. Tests cover shared function, shared statement, and shared type scenarios. All 23 extract tests pass, full verify passes.
collectDependencies now distinguishes toCopy from toRemove — shared private helpers/statements are copied to destination but retained in source, preventing breakage of remaining functions

name: splitCompound rejects literal backticks in quoted arguments
acceptanceCriteria: `assist cli-hook check "assist backlog add-phase 48 \"title\" --task \"Install \`react-router\` as a dependency\""` is parsed successfully (not rejected as "unable to parse")
Literal backticks inside quoted arguments are parsed successfully
Actual backtick command substitution (e.g. echo `whoami`) is still rejected
Existing splitCompound tests continue to pass
description:
The backtick check in splitCompound (`/`.+`/.test(t)`) rejects any token containing backticks, even when they are literal characters inside quoted strings (e.g. markdown formatting in --task values), not shell command substitution.

**Repro:**
1. Run a command with backticks inside quoted arguments, e.g.:
   `assist backlog add-phase 48 "title" --task "Install \`react-router\` as a dependency"`
2. The cli-hook check rejects it with "not approved (unable to parse)"

**Expected:** Literal backticks inside quoted arguments should be treated as safe — they are not command substitution.

**Actual:** `splitCompound` returns `undefined`, causing the command to be rejected as unparseable.

comments.text: Replaced token-level backtick regex with raw-command scanner using QUOTED_OR_BACKTICK_RE. Extracted hasUnquotedBackticks to its own file to keep splitCompound.ts maintainability above threshold.

Moved backtick detection from parsed tokens to raw command string using a regex that skips quoted strings and escape sequences. Extracted hasUnquotedBackticks to its own file. Quoted backticks are now allowed; unquoted backtick substitution is still rejected. All 31 tests pass including 3 new ones.
Review passed: all 4 acceptance criteria verified — quoted backticks allowed, unquoted backticks still rejected, 31/31 tests pass, cli-hook e2e check passes.
Added hasUnquotedBackticks helper that skips escaped, single-quoted, and double-quoted backticks — only unquoted backticks are rejected as command substitution.

name: backlog ls crashes when jsonl contains legacy verify field on plan tasks
acceptanceCriteria: backlog ls succeeds on backlog files containing the legacy verify field on plan tasks
Unknown keys in plan tasks are stripped rather than causing a crash
description:
**Repro:**
1. Have a project with `.assist/backlog.jsonl` containing plan tasks with a `verify` field (created before the field was removed)
2. Run `assist backlog ls`

**Expected:** The backlog lists successfully, stripping or ignoring the legacy `verify` field.

**Actual:** ZodError crash: `Unrecognized key: "verify"` — all backlog commands are unusable.

`planTaskSchema` uses `z.strictObject` which rejects unknown keys. The `verify` field was removed from this schema, but existing `.assist/backlog.jsonl` files still contain it. When `importFromJsonlIfNeeded` re-parses the jsonl, the strict schema throws and crashes.

comments.text: Changed planTaskSchema from z.strictObject to z.object().strip() so unknown keys (like legacy verify) are silently removed instead of crashing.
Changed planTaskSchema from z.strictObject to z.object().strip() — unknown keys like legacy verify are now silently stripped instead of causing a ZodError crash.
Review: both ACs pass. planTaskSchema changed from strictObject to z.object().strip(), which silently drops unknown keys like verify.
Changed planTaskSchema from strictObject to z.object().strip() so legacy verify fields (and any other unknown keys) are silently dropped instead of crashing.

name: Migration from YAML backlog doesn't update .gitignore
acceptanceCriteria: When .assist/backlog.db is created (via migration or openDb), .assist/backlog.db, .assist/backlog.db-shm, and .assist/backlog.db-wal are added to .gitignore if not already present
Existing .gitignore content is preserved
Works for both migration from YAML and fresh backlog init
description:
**Repro:**
1. Have a project using `assist.backlog.yml` (committed to git)
2. Run any backlog command that triggers the migration to `.assist/backlog.db`
3. Run `git status`

**Expected:** `.assist/backlog.db` and its WAL files are added to `.gitignore` automatically.

**Actual:** `.gitignore` is not updated. The SQLite database and WAL files appear as untracked changes.

comments.text: ensureGitignore extracted to its own file to satisfy maintainability threshold
Added ensureGitignore to openDb — appends .assist/backlog.db, -shm, and -wal to .gitignore on first DB open. Extracted to own file for maintainability. Verified via migration in asteroid-logistics.
Review: all 3 acceptance criteria pass. ensureGitignore is called from openDb, covering both migration and fresh init paths. Existing .gitignore content is preserved.
ensureGitignore called from openDb adds .assist/backlog.db, -shm, -wal to .gitignore on db creation, covering both YAML migration and fresh init paths

name: cli-hook check fails to match allowed commands on Mac
acceptanceCriteria: cli-hook check './build.sh' passes when Bash(./build.sh 2>&1) is in the allow list
cli-hook check 'build.sh' also passes (./ prefix normalization)
Existing .ps1 normalization continues to work
description:
**Repro:**
1. Have `"Bash(./build.sh 2>&1)"` in the allow list
2. Run `assist cli-hook check 'build.sh'` — returns `not approved (unrecognised: build.sh)`
3. Run `assist cli-hook check './build.sh'` — returns `not approved (unrecognised: ./build.sh)`

**Expected:** At least `./build.sh` should match the allow list entry `Bash(./build.sh 2>&1)`, and ideally `build.sh` should too (as was fixed for `.ps1` files).

**Actual:** Neither form is recognised as approved.

**Note:** A similar `./` prefix normalization was previously fixed for `build.ps1`. The matching logic may not be extracting/comparing the command correctly from the `Bash(...)` wrapper.

comments.text: Fixed in matchesAllow.ts: normCmd now strips fd redirect suffixes (2>&1, 2>/dev/null) before normalizing ./ prefix, matching how splitCompound strips them from input commands.

Fixed cli-hook check matching by stripping fd redirect suffixes (2>&1, 2>/dev/null) in normCmd within matchesAllow.ts, so allow list entries like Bash(./build.sh 2>&1) now match both ./build.sh and build.sh inputs. Added 3 tests covering redirect stripping with ./ normalization.

Fixed cli-hook check matching: normCmd now strips fd redirects (2>&1, 2>/dev/null) and ./ prefix from both allow-list entries and input commands, so build.sh and ./build.sh both match Bash(./build.sh 2>&1)

name: devlog --all catch-up mode
acceptanceCriteria: /devlog --all generates devlog entries for every unversioned day before today
Trivial days are automatically skipped (via assist devlog skip) without prompting
The loop stops when the next unversioned day is today or there are no more unversioned days
/devlog without --all continues to work as before (single day)
description: Extend the /devlog skill to accept a --all argument that loops through all unversioned days up to (excluding) today, automatically skipping trivial days.

comments.text: Updated devlog skill prompt: added allowed_args frontmatter for --all, added $ARGUMENTS parsing to route between single-day and catch-up modes, added catch-up loop instructions that repeat next/analyze/skip cycle until target date is today or no unversioned days remain, trivial days are auto-skipped without prompting in --all mode
Added --all catch-up mode to /devlog command. Loops through all unversioned days before today, auto-skipping trivial days and creating entries for non-trivial ones.

Ensure trivial days are automatically skipped without prompting in --all mode

On subsequent iterations (after completing an item), always show the selection prompt so the user confirms before starting the last remaining item.

name: backlog done allows completion with pending phases
acceptanceCriteria: assist backlog done <id> fails with an error when the item has phases that are not yet completed
A clear message lists the pending phase(s)
Completing an item with all phases done continues to work as before
description:
**Repro:**
1. Have a backlog item with a plan containing multiple phases
2. Add a new phase via `assist backlog add-phase`
3. Run `assist backlog done <id>` without completing the new phase

**Expected:** The command rejects completion and warns that pending phases remain.

**Actual:** The item is marked as done despite having uncompleted phases.

comments.text: done

name: next signal terminates wrong Claude session
acceptanceCriteria: C:/Program Files/Git/next signal is scoped to the session that wrote it (e.g. via session ID)
Other running Claude sessions are not affected by another session's signal
Normal /next chaining continues to work when only one session is active
description:
**Repro:**
1. Have multiple Claude sessions running (e.g. one spawned by `assist refine`, another standalone)
2. Run `/next` in one session to write the signal file
3. A different Claude session picks up the signal and terminates

**Expected:** The `/next` signal only affects the session it was invoked from.

**Actual:** A different Claude session is terminated by the signal.

comments.text: Fix: scoped signal file by session ID — getSignalPath() now produces .assist-signal-{sessionId}.json so each session watches/writes its own file, eliminating cross-session interference

Scoped signal file by session ID: getSignalPath() now embeds ASSIST_SESSION_ID in the filename (.assist-signal-{pid}.json). Removed redundant session ID filtering from watchForMarker since file-path scoping handles isolation. Each session watches only its own signal file, preventing cross-session termination.

assist run with no arguments shows the same list alongside the error

name: Signal files with session ID not gitignored
acceptanceCriteria: gitignore entry covers both .assist-signal.json and .assist-signal-<sessionId>.json variants
No signal files appear in git status after a backlog run
description:
**Repro:**
1. Run `assist backlog run` (which spawns a session with a PID-based signal file)
2. Run `git status`

**Expected:** Signal files are not shown as untracked — they are transient IPC files.

**Actual:** `.assist-signal-<pid>.json` appears as untracked because `.gitignore` only has the literal `.assist-signal.json`, not the wildcard `.assist-signal-*.json` pattern (which is already used for `.assist-lock-*.json`).

comments.text: Added .assist-signal*.json and .assist-lock-*.json to ensureGitignore entries so target repos get the wildcard patterns automatically.
Added signal and lock wildcard patterns to ensureGitignore so target repos automatically get .assist-signal*.json and .assist-lock-*.json in their .gitignore
Review: both ACs pass. .gitignore pattern widened from literal to wildcard; ensureGitignore updated to propagate both signal and lock patterns to new repos.
Widened .gitignore pattern from literal .assist-signal.json to wildcard .assist-signal*.json; added signal and lock patterns to ensureGitignore entries.

acceptanceCriteria: assist backlog rewind <id> <phase> --reason "..." sets currentPhase to the given phase index
--reason is required

Rewind is rejected if the target phase is not earlier than the current phase
A comment is added to the item recording the rewind, which phase it was rewound to, and the reason
The run command resumes from the rewound phase on next execution
description: Allow agents and users to rewind a backlog item to an earlier phase, setting currentPhase back to the specified index. The item status is set to in-progress so execution can resume from that phase.
